meta.race_name: Midnite: Built For 2026 Not 2006 Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

rankings.name: Caliban
key_positive_factors: Lightly raced profile with an impressive all-the-way novice win at Kempton
Yard (Andrew Balding) is currently in exceptional form with an elite strike rate
Significant ability indicated by top-rated RPR of 94 relative to OR of 82
key_risks: Making handicap debut which presents a sterner tactical challenge
Current market price (10/11) offers no value for a horse stepping into deeper water

rankings.name: Charlie's Choice
key_positive_factors: Seems better than ever while bidding for a hat-trick of victories
Recent wins over 1m4f and 1m5f suggest he will be very strong in the final furlong
Maintaining a high performance level with a recent RPR of 92
key_risks: Stepping up to Class 4 company after winning in lower grades
Now 3lb higher in the weights than his most recent success

rankings.name: Everyoneknowsadave
key_positive_factors: Proven course specialist with dual victories at Southwell including over C&D
Large RPR-OR gap (+18) suggests he is significantly better than his current mark
Latest effort here in a warm 13-runner handicap shows he is competitive at this level
key_risks: Returning from a 110-day break; may lack sharpness against race-fit rivals
Older runner facing unexposed and potentially better-handicapped 4-year-olds

rankings.name: Sir William
key_positive_factors: Highly consistent profile for former yard; winner at 1m2f and proven on AW
Charlie Johnston stable is historically very effective on the Southwell Tapeta
Capable of matching these on his best form if fully wound up for his debut
key_risks: Returning from a 5-month absence; market support is crucial to gauge intent
Starting out for a new yard; often takes a run to reach peak fitness

rankings.name: Midnight Rumble
key_positive_factors: Running off a 2lb lower mark than when a close fifth at Wolverhampton
Latest RPR of 91 confirms he is still performing near his peak
Luke Catton's 3lb claim is a tactical advantage in a handicap of this class
key_risks: On a long losing run; often finds one or two too good in the finish
Can be vulnerable to rivals with more turn-of-foot at this distance

rankings.name: Crackalackin
key_positive_factors: Back down in trip to 1m3f after fading late over 1m6f at Chelmsford
Change of headgear (indicated by spotlight) could unlock further improvement
Respectable RPR of 91 suggests he is not out of his depth in this grade
key_risks: Plateauing form figures (414-4) suggest he is struggling to improve past his mark
Tendency to fade in the closing stages is a major concern in a Class 4 handicap

pace_analysis.insight: Caliban's front-running style combined with Charlie Johnston's tendency to send Sir William forward suggests a contested lead that will likely set the race up for a strong finisher like Charlie's Choice.
